purpose: Day-7 SECOND INDEPENDENT external axis: mouse-knockout / model-organism phenotype (IMPC/MGI via Open Targets mousePhenotypes + NOD-mouse PubMed literature). Orthogonal to the gate's human-genetics/eQTL logic.
axis_independence: The gate/adjudication rule used {curated immune-GO, OT human T1D>T2D association, GWAS T2D-tag, measured Treg direction, human cis-eQTL}. It NEVER used mouse-KO phenotype. IMPC/MGI germline/conditional KO phenotype is generated in an orthogonal model organism => a validation that can fail.

data_source.primary: Open Targets target.mousePhenotypes (aggregates IMPC + MGI); label rule over Mammalian-Phenotype top-level classes.
data_source.secondary: PubMed NOD-mouse / autoimmune-diabetes literature (independent of IMPC/MGI curation).

coverage.note: 2 of the 5 zero-record genes are GO genes (CD226, CD6); both are independently confirmed by NOD-mouse literature (see nod_literature).

secondary_any_recordonly.note: y_any near-saturated (33/39 pos) -> weaker discrimination, CI touches 0

interpretation.both_baselines_below_0.5: OT-association and eQTL-count baselines both score AUROC<0.5 against the mouse-immune axis because they are elevated by metabolic T1D/T2D genes (NDUF*, HNF1A, KCNQ1, CDKAL1...) that carry human eQTLs but NO mouse immune phenotype. The gate's function-aware WITHHOLD of those genes is what produces its positive AUROC.
interpretation.GO_arm_precision: Of 14 GO genes with an IMPC/MGI record, 13 (93%) have a mouse immune/hematopoietic phenotype (only UBASH3A lacks one, nrows=5). The 2 GO genes without an IMPC record (CD226, CD6) are both confirmed by NOD-mouse literature. => gate GO calls are highly specific to genes with an orthogonal mouse immune phenotype.
interpretation.honest_false_negatives: 15 WITHHELD genes DO have a mouse immune phenotype (CCR7, CD5, GATA3, IKZF1, IKZF3, NOTCH2, RASGRP1, RUNX1, SOCS1, ZAP70, plus metabolic-pleiotropy genes FTO, IRS1, PDX1, PPARG, TCF7L2). This is the gate's conservatism: it withholds unless its human-genetics+eQTL+direction rule fires, so genuinely immune genes lacking a firing human signal in THIS screen are withheld. Gate sensitivity vs the broad mouse-immune axis is only 0.46. This is reported plainly, not hidden.

trio.CD226: GO; no IMPC record BUT NOD lit n=38, incl. PMID 39636437 'Inhibition of CD226 co-stimulation suppresses diabetes development in the NOD mouse by augmenting Tregs' -> direct orthogonal confirmation.
trio.RASGRP1: WITHHELD by gate; HAS mouse immune phenotype (54 IMPC rows) + NOD/T1D genetics lit (PMID 19465406). => a gate false-negative on the mouse axis; honest.
trio.PRKCQ: GO; IMPC immune phenotype present (34 rows) + NOD lit n=8. -> concordant.

CD226.query: CD226 AND (NOD mouse OR nonobese diabetic OR autoimmune diabetes OR insulitis)

decision: CONCORDANT (powered): gate AUROC 0.687 > best function-blind baseline 0.453; advantage +0.231, bootstrap 95% CI [0.056,0.416] excludes 0; permutation p=0.030. n=39 with 28 positives clears the pre-registered power floor (>=8 records, >=4 positives). Robust in the no-distractor sensitivity set (+0.290, CI [0.095,0.518]).
honest_caveats: Gate is high-specificity / modest-sensitivity against a BROAD mouse-immune axis (spec 0.91, sens 0.46): it earns its AUROC by correctly WITHHOLDING metabolic genes, not by recovering every immune gene. 15 withheld genes have mouse immune phenotypes — reported as honest false negatives.
y_mouse_any is near-saturated (33/39) so its discrimination is weaker and its CI touches 0.

Mouse-immune label is a coarse ontology-class flag, not a curated autoimmune-diabetes phenotype; it is deliberately orthogonal but not T1D-specific. NOD-literature axis supplies the T1D-specific confirmation for key genes.
The 5 zero-IMPC-record genes were not counted as negatives in the powered headline; they are flagged separately.
